samples.raw_output:
```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.55,
    "corrections": [
        "Hi, I had a similar problem.",
        "Here are the tips that helped me.",
        "You can find these apps at Play Market.",
        "Don't spend a lot of time on TV and social media.",
        "You should leave useless groups on VK, don't watch YouTube a lot!",
        "It will bring you enough energy.",
        "I hope my tips will help you.",
        "For more information, you can write to me in personal messages."
    ],
    "feedback": "Your text is well-organized with a clear structure, using a numbered list to present your ideas effectively. You successfully communicated your advice, which is the main goal. However, there are frequent errors in basic grammar, such as subject-verb agreement ('Here is the tips'), plural demonstratives ('this apps'), and prepositions ('time to TV', 'at VK'). Spelling mistakes like 'simular' and 'enought' also affect the accuracy. With more attention to these basic rules, your writing will become much stronger."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.75,
    "corrections": [
        "decition -> decision",
        "tips how to get it -> tips on how to get it",
        "options how to organize -> options on how to organize",
        "each my day gets easier -> each of my days gets easier",
        "other's works -> others' work",
        "But very important thing -> But a very important thing"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a strong B2 text. You have organized your ideas logically and used a good range of cohesive devices (e.g., 'However', 'Firstly', 'For example', 'Also') to structure your argument. The vocabulary is varied and appropriate for the context, including phrases like 'overload yourself', 'abuse this help', and 'long periods of free time'. You successfully use complex sentence structures, such as 'Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok...', which shows a good command of the language. While there are some errors in spelling ('decition'), prepositions ('tips how'), and word order ('each my day'), these do not obscure the meaning and are consistent with the B2 level."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "I want to say you abot -> I want to tell you about",
        "two my own tips -> two of my own tips",
        "what help me -> which help me",
        "reccommend more communicate -> recommend communicating more",
        "look to life simplie -> look at life simply",
        "In futcher -> In the future",
        "networking give you -> networking gives you",
        "a lot opportunity -> a lot of opportunities",
        "need count -> need to count",
        "think what you study -> think that you study",
        "hasn't free time -> don't have free time",
        "try note -> try noting",
        "habbits -> habits",
        "Delite -> Delete"
    ],
    "feedback": "The text demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. You have successfully organized your ideas using linking words ('Firstly', 'Secondly', 'Also') and attempted sophisticated vocabulary such as 'networking', 'lifestyle', and 'motivation', which is commendable. However, the text contains a high frequency of spelling errors ('reccommend', 'futcher', 'beleave') and basic grammar mistakes, particularly with verb patterns ('recommend more communicate', 'need count') and word order. While your meaning is generally clear, these errors indicate a gap between your vocabulary range and your grammatical control. Focus on reviewing spelling and practicing correct verb forms (e.g., 'recommend doing' vs. 'recommend to do')."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "Hellow -> Hello",
        "and me to -> and me too",
        "In first semester -> In the first semester",
        "wery -> very",
        "how can I fix it -> how I can fix it",
        "I will give you them -> I will give them to you",
        "some task-management system -> a task-management system",
        "in uni -> at uni",
        "enouth -> enough",
        "bust -> boost"
    ],
    "feedback": "Your text is well-organized and clear, particularly with the use of a numbered list to structure your advice. You demonstrate good vocabulary for this level, using words like 'prioritize', 'productivity', and 'task-management system', which shows you are moving towards B2. However, your accuracy holds the score back, as there are frequent spelling mistakes ('wery', 'enouth', 'bust') and errors with basic grammar structures like indirect questions ('how can I fix') and object pronouns ('give you them'). Focus on proofreading your spelling and reviewing word order in complex sentences."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "It is easily. -> It is easy.",
        "Now I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier. -> Now I want to give some tips to make your difficult studies easier.",
        "It is not obviously tip -> It is not an obvious tip",
        "your brain relax and do not do anything -> your brain relaxes and does not do anything",
        "solve spend less time -> spend less time"
    ],
    "feedback": "Your text is well-organized with clear signposting (Tip number one, two, etc.), which shows good writing strategy. You successfully attempt complex sentences to explain your reasoning (e.g., 'If you sleep more than six hours, you wake up and understand...'), which places you firmly in the B1 level. However, there are frequent errors with word forms (adjectives vs. adverbs, e.g., 'easily' instead of 'easy') and sentence structure. While your meaning is generally clear, improving your grammatical accuracy will help you express these good ideas more effectively."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.65,
    "corrections": [
        "I think, that -> I think that",
        "TODO list -> to-do list",
        "Also you can -> Also, you can",
        "for TODO list -> for the to-do list",
        "this tips -> these tips",
        "would't -> won't"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a solid B1 level text. You have organized your ideas well using bullet points and linking words like 'Firstly' and 'So', which makes your advice easy to follow. Your vocabulary is appropriate for the topic, with good use of terms like 'productive', 'concentrate', and 'satisfied'. However, there are some recurring grammar errors, such as 'this tips' (should be 'these tips') and issues with conditional structures ('If you stick... you wouldn't'). Spelling and punctuation need attention, particularly with 'would't' and the capitalization of 'TODO'. Overall, the message is clear and communicative."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.7,
    "corrections": [
        "Everyone got same time a day -> Everyone has the same amount of time in a day",
        "study good -> study well",
        "deadlines to be done -> deadlines to meet",
        "homeworks -> homework",
        "Also Its important -> Also, it's important",
        "If subject seems not intresting -> If a subject seems not interesting",
        "its not needed -> it's not needed",
        "get max grade -> get the maximum grade"
    ],
    "feedback": "Your text demonstrates a good range of B2-level vocabulary (e.g., 'allocate', 'exhausted', 'priorities', 'deadlines') and successfully employs complex sentence structures, particularly the rhetorical question regarding prioritization. The text is well-organized with clear discourse markers ('To sum up', 'Also'). While your meaning is clear, there are frequent grammar errors, particularly with articles ('Everyone got same time', 'If subject'), adverbial forms ('study good'), and uncountable nouns ('homeworks'). Focusing on accuracy will help you reach the next level."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.8,
    "corrections": [
        "I cannnot agree -> I cannot agree",
        "there is different situation -> there is a different situation",
        "in other difficult area -> in other difficult areas",
        "In the university -> At university",
        "knowledge that university gives -> the knowledge that a university gives"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a strong B2 essay. You have organized your ideas very well, using a clear structure with an introduction, contrasting arguments, and a conclusion. You used effective discourse markers ('Of course', 'To sum it up') and complex sentence structures ('...field in which they want to work'). The arguments are logical and fully developed. While there are some errors with articles and plural forms, they do not impede communication. To reach the next level, focus on refining your use of definite/indefinite articles and ensuring noun-phrase agreement."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "our planet has became -> our planet has become",
        "Does this pollutions -> Does this pollution",
        "It cause politicians do not look -> It causes politicians not to look",
        "People who cares -> People who care",
        "significantly overreacting -> are significantly overreacting",
        "climate changes is -> climate change is",
        "only people responsible -> only people are responsible",
        "making Earth more clear -> making Earth cleaner"
    ],
    "feedback": "Your essay demonstrates a good attempt at an argumentative structure, successfully organizing your thoughts into four distinct paragraphs as required. You have employed some sophisticated vocabulary and specific terms like 'eco-activists', 'vital', and 'observing', which shows a good lexical range for this topic. You also effectively used rhetorical questions to engage the reader. However, there are frequent grammar errors, particularly with verb forms (e.g., 'has became', 'It cause') and subject-verb agreement ('People who cares'). While these errors are noticeable, they do not block the communication of your complex ideas. With improved grammatical accuracy, your writing would be very strong."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.75,
    "corrections": [
        "part in becoming -> part of becoming",
        "werelucky -> were lucky",
        "talant -> talent",
        "alwaysbeats -> always beats",
        "On my point of view -> From my point of view / In my opinion"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a strong B2 essay. You have presented a well-structured argument with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion. The use of discourse markers such as 'However', 'On the other hand', and 'All in all' is effective and demonstrates good organisational skills. You also use some advanced vocabulary ('fundamental', 'self-assured', 'rely on') and complex sentence structures successfully. The main areas for improvement are spelling (e.g., 'talent' instead of 'talant') and prepositional phrases ('From my point of view' rather than 'On my point of view'). These errors do not impede communication but frequent spelling mistakes prevent a higher score."
}
```
